key_points: Tercero dorms placed next to active cow fields — smell is real
Mixed-gender room assignments have occurred without student consent
Off-campus housing runs $700+/month; affordability is a genuine concern
key_quotes: I remembered having to live at the tercero dorm my freshman year...next to the cow shit. They should've given students a discount for having to smell that shit.
Its a double set up, where 4 doubles share a kitchen and bathroom, however its 7 guys and 1 girl.
Affordable housing can be found at UC Davis, unlike UC Berkeley...Be sure to get a secure lock for your bicycle as bike thefts are common.

key_points: Not UCSB, but freshman dorm floors are often very social and active
Party scene exists — non-Greek students report getting into parties regularly
Davis is bad at advertising events; you must actively seek things out
key_quotes: Most of the stuff I thought it would take my whole college life to experience all happened within like 2 months.
I partied almost every weekend and it wasn't hard to find a good time going down somewhere. I'm not in Greek life, but I still got into every party.
Davis is horrible at letting you know about events, or they'll spam your inbox until you start ignoring their emails, no in between.

key_points: Dining commons are functional but not a standout feature students rave about
Local spots like Preethi's Indian Cuisine and the now-closed Crepe Bistro inspire strong loyalty
Yolo Lo Berry on campus sparked genuine student excitement; food scene is improving
key_quotes: My go-to comfort meal after a long week :((( rip crepe bistro i miss u every day.
Preethi's Indian Cuisine in downtown Davis catered the entire memorial and refused to accept payment from us.
Will it be equally as expensive [as the crepe place]?

key_points: Students openly discuss depression, financial stress, and academic burnout on Reddit
Multiple posts reference taking leaves of absence or nearly dropping out due to mental health
Campus resources exist but students navigating them solo, especially those with controlling parents
key_quotes: I'm currently taking a 'temporary' leave from UC Davis due to stress and mental health, and possibly leaving permanently.
What can I do in a situation where I am 22 and a dependent on my parents' insurance, but they are highly against me seeking out therapy?
I didn't just have homesickness; I genuinely didn't like it and have felt like this since I arrived.

key_points: Aid packages can worsen year-over-year with no clear explanation
Out-of-state students get essentially no financial aid — costs balloon fast
Students have resorted to sham marriages and community college transfers to manage costs
key_quotes: I don't think I'm gonna make the school year. I do not have the financial backing to cover tuition, and my aid package somehow got worse than last year.
I am an out of state student so no financial aid, and my mom recently had a stroke.
Go to community college. UCD lower division classes are garbage [and it saves money].

key_points: Research university first — TAs grade heavily, professors prioritize research over teaching
Quarter system is brutal; little time to recover from a bad start
Unique majors like Wildlife Biology and tractor driving are genuine differentiators
key_quotes: I came here for a quarter and left and transferred to a cal state. My professors generally didn't care about teaching. It's a research school first.
Everywhere I look yall got ChatGPT open. Genuinely wth is wrong with yall. Whats the point of a top level university if yall dont want to learn.
A degree in the sciences or engineering from UC Davis really means something out in the real world.

key_points: Slow to communicate — COVID reopening plans announced months after peer schools
ASUCD hid critical student comments and reported critics anonymously on YikYak
Gary May received a 30% pay raise while campus workers went on strike
key_quotes: THEY ARE SLOW TO ANNOUNCE THINGS. Other schools got it in May. Davis students didn't know until July.
ASUCD is doing everything they can to hide their embarrassments.
Reminder that Gary May got a 30% pay increase while other UCD workers go on strike for pennies.

key_points: Small, flat, bike-friendly town — genuinely loved by most, isolating for others
Sacramento is 15 minutes away; free bus for students makes it accessible
Campus is beautiful but the SS&H 'Death Star' building is a notorious maze
key_quotes: Davis really felt like a college town. I am so glad I went here over UC Berkeley.
Getting lost at/being late to an appointment because of the SS&H labyrinth is an unofficial rite of passage at UCD.
This place is boring, no lie. But I found friends that I really really love.

key_points: Strong ROI for STEM and pre-health — ranked 11th among California schools for 10-year median ROI
Tech sector placement is weak compared to UCSD or Berkeley; minimal Silicon Valley pipeline
Research publication opportunities as an undergrad are genuinely accessible
key_quotes: Tech wise tho, like you mentioned, I'd agree, I didn't know very many folks who were in that sector.
I was able to publish my first research project from working in one [of Davis's research positions]!
If you stay in CA, UC Davis on a resume is Strong. I always got the interview.

key_points: In-state tuition (~$40k total cost) is competitive for a top-40 research university
Out-of-state students pay dramatically more with little aid — poor value vs. in-state peers
Study abroad via UCEAP to Asia can actually be cheaper than Davis tuition
key_quotes: The tuition for me to go to Korea/Japan was actually cheaper than my tuition at UC Davis.
I literally did this [married for FAFSA] between my sophomore and junior year of college. Saved me about $20k a year.
I went to the cheapest option, which was UC Davis. I received a large scholarship.

key_points: Collaborative, down-to-earth, and low-ego compared to Berkeley or UCLA
Campus mascot Cheeto the cat has hundreds of posts — says everything about the culture
Politically active and progressive; frequent protests and strong community advocacy
key_quotes: Davis is dope. Very inclusive and everyone is more than willing to collaborate on coursework.
Great university. Even better people.
I always tell them... 'Great university. Even better people.'

key_points: **Academic Integrity:** A paid proxy admitted attending class and exams in-person for a student who then fled to Cornell without paying.
**Animal Abuse:** Alpha Gamma Rho fraternity filmed members shooting ducks with guns; police and animal control responded.
**Data Privacy:** UC Davis likely handed student personal data to the federal government citing antisemitism investigations, without notifying affected students.
key_quotes: He hired me through an agency to take two UC Davis classes for him. I attended class, sat for the midterms and finals in person.
AGR members chasing ducks with rifles while saying 'stupid fucking ducks' and another member walking by with a deceased duck.
According to the article UC Davis likely did the same [as Berkeley], but has not yet informed the affected students.

key_points: UCEAP study abroad to Asia can cost less than a quarter at Davis — wildly underused
Unitrans student-run bus system employs students and is beloved by alumni
Professor Enderle and Dr. Hayashi are cult-favorite professors worth seeking out
key_quotes: I did the UCEAP program to Korea and Japan. I did it twice because it was a life changing experience. And the tuition was actually cheaper than my tuition at UC Davis.
If you work for Unitrans, your experience will be more amazing!
omg my first semester at Davis, he [Enderle] dressed as bing bong from inside out and frolicked through the lecture hall, pelting students with colorful plastic balls.
